description_long: This kiosk features a robust, angular structure with a predominantly red exterior and a wide, slanted front control panel. The front face is equipped with a large orange display screen, a grid of square buttons, a rotary dial telephone handset, toggle switches, and a prominent red emergency button, all set into a gray panel. The sides are solid and unadorned, with a slight inward curve at the base, while the rear is completely flat and featureless. The top surface has two vent-like grilles, suggesting internal electronics or ventilation. The materials appear to be primarily metal with some plastic components for the controls and display. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century industrial or control room equipment, with a utilitarian and slightly retro aesthetic.
description: A red, angular kiosk with a control panel featuring a display, buttons, switches, and a telephone handset, evoking a retro industrial style.
description_view_0: Left side view showing the kiosk's solid red panel and the angled control surface extending outward.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the control panel with a screen, buttons, switches, a telephone handset, and an emergency button.
description_view_2: Right side view mirroring the left, with the angled control surface and solid red panel visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain, flat red surface with no visible features or openings.
attribute_signage_type: digital display integrated into the front control panel; no visible printed or illuminated signage
attribute_roof_design: flat roof with two rectangular vent grilles, constructed from painted metal
